sourceMovie.title: Domino
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Domino works as a action movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The action and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Mesrine: Killer Instinct
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Domino clicked for you, Mesrine: Killer Instinct (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Domino?
faq.answer: Try Mesrine: Killer Instinct, Run All Night, 16 Blocks—each shares DNA with Domino while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Domino?
faq.answer: Domino (2005) is best described as Action, Crime, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Domino worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
